abstract: This article examines the principles of data acquisition and entry compilation for the crowdsourced dictionary of neologisms developed at the University of Warsaw. The project is situated within the broader landscape of contemporary lexicography, drawing comparisons to existing neologism databases and other community-driven linguistic resources. Furthermore, the author outlines the sociolinguistic factors driving the emergence and adoption of new lexical units while addressing the inherent terminological ambiguity surrounding the concept of the ‘neologism’ itself.
